entries.title: US Partially Lifts Anthropic Model Ban — Mythos 5 Restored to Critical Infrastructure Defenders; Fable 5 Return Expected 'As Soon as This Week'
entries.summary: Commerce Secretary Lutnick's June 26 letter to Anthropic restored Mythos 5 access to roughly 100+ US critical infrastructure organizations; Axios reported June 27 that insiders expect Fable 5 restrictions to lift 'as soon as this coming week,' marking a major de-escalation — though Fable 5 remains formally banned pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off, and European allies have expressed frustration at their new dependence on Washington for frontier AI model access.

entries.title: GPT-5.6 System Card Details Frontier Cybersecurity Evals — Sol Competitive With Mythos on ExploitBench — as OpenAI Formally Objects to Government Reviews Becoming Permanent
entries.summary: OpenAI's June 26 system card for GPT-5.6 Sol reports 91.9% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and competitive performance with Anthropic's Mythos Preview on ExploitBench at only ~1/3 the output tokens; simultaneously, OpenAI published a formal objection stating it 'doesn't believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,' the first clear public pushback from a frontier lab against the emerging pre-release government review regime.

entries.title: California AI-Enabled Gas Price Fixing Class Action: BP, Circle K, Walmart and Others Sued Over Kalibrate Algorithm (June 22)
entries.summary: Three California drivers filed a federal class action on June 22 against BP, Circle K, Marathon Petroleum, 7-Eleven, Walmart, Albertsons, and AI pricing software firm Kalibrate, alleging use of Kalibrate's AI-based Fuel Pricing platform to illegally coordinate pump prices — violating California's Cartwright Act and the new AB 325 algorithmic price-fixing law (effective January 2026) — in a case that could push prices down as much as 30 cents per gallon and signals a new wave of AI antitrust enforcement.

entries.title: Colorado's Original AI Act Reaches Its June 30 Effective Date Already Repealed — The Nation's First Comprehensive State AI Law Never Took Effect
entries.summary: Colorado's original AI Act (SB 24-205) — the first US state law to require duty-of-care, impact assessments, and anti-discrimination protections for high-risk AI, and the specific target of Trump's December 2025 EO calling for state preemption — was repealed and replaced by a narrower disclosure-only framework (SB 189, signed May 14) effective January 1, 2027, before its June 30, 2026 effective date arrived, suggesting that ambitious EU-style comprehensive state AI regulation may not survive the current US political environment.

links.title: Hunton — Colorado AI Act Amended and Effective Date Delayed

entries.title: Government Frontier Model Access Is Now a De Facto Regime — Trump EO Formal Framework (August 1 Deadline) Already Outpaced by Ad Hoc Reviews
entries.summary: The 60-day deadline of Trump's June 2 EO for agencies to build a classified benchmarking process for 'covered frontier models' and a voluntary pre-release framework falls August 1, 2026, yet the informal government reviews of Fable 5/Mythos 5 and GPT-5.6 have already operationalized government control over frontier model releases in practice — with Commerce Secretary Lutnick's letter to Anthropic explicitly noting that Anthropic 'committed to work with the US government on protocols, standards and releases,' cementing a new governance reality that Semafor described as 'a new regulatory regime that gives the US government control over the release of frontier AI models.'
